rationale: Title Y is stronger overall because it names the registry, population size, and clinically important endpoints. Its added specificity makes the study’s contribution clearer for JACC readers, even though it is longer than the leaner JACC-style Title X.
loser_weakness: Title X is clean and journal-appropriate, but it is too generic. It identifies the comparison and data source, yet omits the acute success and AV block outcomes that make the study clinically meaningful.
